news.headline: FBI Director Kash Patel fires back at drinking allegations
news.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel and Senator Chris Van Hollen had a heated exchange during a Senate budget hearing.

news.headline: Peru presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez charged with financial crimes
news.summary: Prosecutor calls for leftist candidate to be jailed for five years and four months over false financial disclosures.

news.headline: Starmer at risk because he pushed Labour to be ‘new Conservative Party’
news.summary: Author Oliver Eagleton explains why British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is fighting for his job.

news.headline: Iran war live: Trump travels to China as conflict with Tehran looms large
news.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed 2,883 people and injured 8,787 since March 2, Lebanon's Health Ministry says.

news.headline: Chinese firm unveils ‘transformer’ style manned robot
news.summary: Unitree Robotics has released footage of its CEO piloting the GD01 transformable mecha that smashes through walls.

news.headline: Honduras mayor arrested for masterminding environmentalist’s killing
news.summary: Adan Funez was one of three men arrested this week over alleged involvement in the shooting of activist Juan Lopez.

news.headline: Sam Altman says Elon Musk wanted 90 percent of OpenAI in high-stakes trial
news.summary: Landmark trial could shape future and leadership of OpenAI as company prepares for possible initial public offering.

news.headline: Basketball star Brandon Clarke, Memphis Grizzlies forward, dies at age 29
news.summary: Neither the Grizzlies nor Clarke’s agency, Priority Sports, immediately released details surrounding his death.

news.headline: Trump says he doesn’t need China’s help on Iran war as he heads to Beijing
news.summary: Trump gives conflicting messages on prominence of Iran war in upcoming talks, with his administration emphasising trade.

news.headline: US court pauses decision blocking Trump’s 10 percent global tariff
news.summary: A coalition of 24 states have argued that Trump's latest tariffs do not meet the standards of 1974 Trade Act.

news.headline: Cannes juror denounces Hollywood boycott of actors for Gaza war views
news.summary: 2026 Cannes Film Festival juror Paul Laverty denounced Hollywood for boycotting actors who’ve opposed the Gaza war.

news.headline: Trump backs Pakistan as Iran mediator after criticism from Lindsey Graham
news.summary: US president lauds Islamabad, but his Republican ally says he does not trust Pakistan to facilitate Iran diplomacy.

news.headline: Why have peace efforts failed to end conflict in Sudan?
news.summary: The UN says the use of drones is making the conflict more dangerous.

news.headline: Red Cross says people displaced by conflict in Colombia doubled last year
news.summary: Annual report by ICRC finds worsening conditions for Colombian civilians amid continued fighting between armed groups.

news.headline: Why is Iran increasingly targeting the UAE in its war messaging?
news.summary: The UAE's close ties to the US and Israel have placed the Gulf country under Iranian suspicion.

news.headline: Ukraine hits Russia’s distant gas facilities after Moscow’s attacks kill 6
news.summary: The attack comes in retaliation for the Russian attacks that killed at least six in Ukraine, according to the president.

news.headline: Moment taxi driver wrestles armed man during police chase in Turkiye
news.summary: A taxi driver wrestled a gunman that was forcing him to drive during a police chase in Turkiye.

news.headline: Macron unveils $27 billion Africa investment, urges reset with Europe
news.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, in Kenya for the Africa Forward summit, called for a ‘fundamental reset’ in Africa.

news.headline: Trump skirts question on US ‘red lines’ for Iran ceasefire
news.summary: Trump skirted a question from a reporter about whether he has a ‘red line’ that would end the ceasefire with Iran.

news.headline: ‘Cotton picking’: US lawmaker condemned for racist comment about Jeffries
news.summary: US representative Jen Kiggans appears to endorse racist statements about top House Democrat Hakeem Jeffries.

news.headline: World News in Brief: Conflict drives hunger in DR Congo, mass corporal punishment in Afghanistan, Earth’s sand is running out
news.summary: More than 26.5 million people in the Democratic Republic of the Congo are facing acute hunger, according to a new analysis from UN-backed food security experts published Tuesday.

news.headline: Chad’s refugee crisis overwhelms maternity care in east, UN agency warns
news.summary: Many women in eastern Chad are being forced to give birth in overcrowded clinics with limited medicine, minimal equipment and severe shortages of anesthesia, as a worsening humanitarian crisis overwhelms the country’s fragile healthcare system, the UN reproductive health agency, UNFPA, warned on Tue

news.headline: Children shot, stabbed and pepper-sprayed in occupied West Bank
news.summary: Israeli military operations and surging settler attacks in the occupied West Bank are killing and maiming a growing number of Palestinian children, while in Gaza tens of thousands with life-changing injuries lack access to treatment and rehabilitation, UN agencies warned on Tuesday.

news.headline: Middle East: Deadly weekend in Lebanon, continued violence in Gaza
news.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon continues to deteriorate despite the ceasefire announced last month with Israel, the United Nations said on Monday.

news.headline: Armed drones leading cause of civilian death in Sudan war: UN rights chief
news.summary: Drones caused more than 80 per cent of civilian deaths in Sudan’s war during the first four months of 2026, killing at least 880 people, the UN human rights chief said on Monday, warning that escalating drone warfare could push the conflict into an even deadlier phase.&nbsp;

news.headline: Strait of Hormuz de-escalation is urgent, says UN chief
news.summary: As the Strait of Hormuz crisis deepens and tensions between Iran and the United States remain unresolved, oil prices rose again early Monday, prompting the UN Secretary-General to call for a peaceful resolution and warn of the widening fallout across Africa and beyond.

news.headline: UN launches major Nairobi expansion as Guterres highlights Africa’s growing global role
news.summary: The United Nations on Monday launched a major expansion of its Nairobi headquarters, with Secretary-General António Guterres and Kenyan President William Ruto marking what officials described as a significant milestone for the Organization’s presence in Africa.

news.headline: Storm, displacement and hidden wartime explosives deepen emergency in Solomon Islands
news.summary: More than 150,000 people have been affected by Tropical Cyclone Maila, the latest storm in the Pacific area, which continues to drive what the UN relief coordination office OCHA has described as “significant humanitarian needs” across the Solomon Islands.

news.headline: First Person: Surviving death row in Thailand
news.summary: A Thai woman who spent more than 20 years in prison after being found guilty of drugs trafficking – including eight on death row – has told the UN how learning to sew helped her find meaning in life behind bars, and a job when she was released.

news.headline: Nuclear energy in the Middle East: A realistic choice or a risk?
news.summary: As global electricity demand grows, so does the popularity of nuclear energy. In the Middle East, several countries are evaluating or advancing nuclear power projects, balancing weighty issues such as regional security, climatic conditions and international cooperation.

news.headline: Somalia teeters on the brink of catastrophe as hunger crisis deepens
news.summary: “This is not a distant warning. This is a crisis that is unfolding right now and it's deepening quickly.”

news.headline: Lebanon war leaves families foraging for food despite ceasefire
news.summary: The fragile ceasefire in Lebanon hasn’t prevented “ongoing killing and displacement”, with villages in the south of the country rendered completely unrecognizable after Israeli strikes, aid teams reported on Friday.

news.headline: Central African Republic: Funding cuts put humanitarian support at risk
news.summary: The Central African Republic (CAR) is making progress towards stability and security but major aid budget cuts threaten humanitarian operations there, a senior official with the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Friday in New York.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine: Over 3,000 attacks on healthcare since full-scale Russian invasion
news.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified more than 3,000 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN agency reported on Friday.

news.headline: Bahrain and US float Security Council resolution on the Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: Bahrain and the United States have circulated a draft Security Council resolution calling for Iran to cease attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, their ambassadors outlined to journalists at UN Headquarters in New York on Thursday.

news.headline: Lebanon: Fresh strike on Beirut suburbs ‘a very alarming development’
news.summary: An Israeli airstrike overnight on the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital Beirut has sparked a new wave of displacement among civilians already impacted by months of conflict, the United Nations said on Thursday.

news.headline: More than 70 civilians killed in Ukraine in less than a week
news.summary: At least 70 civilians have been killed and more than 500 injured across Ukraine since the start of May, UN human rights monitors said on Wednesday, as waves of attacks hit cities across the country and humanitarian workers struggled to reach communities near the frontline.

news.headline: Security Council holds closed-door talks following attacks on United Arab Emirates
news.summary: As the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz continues, the United Nations reiterates its commitment to support efforts towards peace, UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday during his media briefing from New York.

news.headline: Gaza flotilla: Aid delivery ‘not a crime’, UN rights office tells Israel
news.summary: The UN human rights office on Wednesday called on Israel to immediately release two members of the Global Sumud Flotilla detained in international waters, warning that solidarity with Palestinians and attempts to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza “is not a crime.”

news.headline: World News in Brief: Gulf tensions rise, Gaza health needs ‘staggering’, skills gap threat
news.summary: The UN has expressed deep concern over escalating security incidents in the Gulf, warning that recent attacks risk undermining efforts to maintain regional stability.

news.headline: In Lebanon, the same fears and dangers persist despite ceasefire: UNHCR
news.summary: Death and destruction have continued unabated in Lebanon while communities are still unable to return to their homes despite a ceasefire that began on 17 April, humanitarians said on Tuesday.

news.headline: UN warns of worsening human rights crisis in Mali after deadly attacks
news.summary: The human rights situation in Mali is rapidly deteriorating following coordinated attacks by armed groups across the country, with civilians killed, displaced and cut off from food and aid, UN rights office OHCHR said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Education or food? Funding shortfalls force Sudanese refugees in Egypt to ‘make very difficult decisions’
news.summary: Civilians who have fled the war in Sudan and sought shelter in neighbouring Egypt could potentially face a new battle – the loss of critical services that ensure their survival, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, has warned.&nbsp;

news.headline: Human spread of hantavirus not ruled out on cruise ship
news.summary: Hantavirus victims on a ship in the Atlantic Ocean may have been infected prior to joining the cruise and human-to-human transmission on board cannot be ruled out – although it is rare - the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Global rise of cyberattacks exposes limits of technical solutions
news.summary: The internet reaches into every corner of the world and is vital to everything from health systems and financial markets to public services and election organising. This intense global interconnectedness clearly comes with great benefits, but a cybercrime epidemic is putting millions of lives at ris

news.headline: Middle East: Fighting and displacement in Lebanon; call for ‘sustained access’ in Gaza
news.summary: Lebanon’s fragile ceasefire is being tested by renewed violence and rising humanitarian needs amid continuing civilians displacement, services under pressure and aid cuts.

news.headline: Uncertainty continues over safety in the  Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: Amid claims and counter-claims of strikes and confrontations in the crucial Strait of Hormuz between Iran and the United States, UN maritime officials continue to urge vessels to exercise “maximum caution”.

news.headline: LIVE: 2026 World Press Freedom Day
news.summary: Media freedom is not a given and its absence frequently leaves communities and vulnerable individuals in danger. To mark World Press Freedom Day, we’ll be hearing from UN agencies, along with war correspondents and reporters who face major obstacles trying to do their job. The aim is to get a better

news.headline: UN winds down Türkiye-Syria aid route, after more than 65,000 lifesaving journeys
news.summary: The UN has formally concluded its cross-border humanitarian operations from Türkiye into Syria, marking the end of an 11-year mission that served as one of the most complex supply chains in the organization’s history.&nbsp;

news.headline: Middle East crisis puts aid, food, fuel further out of reach for millions already struggling – UN agencies
news.summary: As the Middle East crisis continues the humanitarian fallout is worsening, with aid route disruptions and food and fuel price hikes wrecking the lives and the rights of the most vulnerable people worldwide, UN agencies warned on Friday.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 1 May: Strain on food, fuel and aid systems continues
news.summary: The strain on food, fuel and aid systems worldwide continues as the Middle East crisis deepens. Rising oil prices, supply disruptions and mounting transport costs are affecting humanitarian operations and access to essential goods far beyond the region. Stay with us this Friday for live updates.

news.headline: ‘Real impact on the ground’ needed as Indonesia backs UN reform
news.summary: Indonesia is expecting a “strengthened multilateral system that delivers real impact on the ground,” as one of the key outcomes of the ongoing reform of the United Nations, that’s according to the country’s outgoing Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs for Multilateral Cooperation, Tri Tharyat.

news.headline: Gang-controlled streets, shuttered newsrooms: How violence is eroding Haiti’s media
news.summary: Journalists working in Haiti are under constant threat of death or injury from rapidly expanding criminal gangs, as they continue to report news and information which they hope will help keep fellow citizens safe.

news.headline: Lebanon strikes deepen crisis as hunger rises and conditions worsen in Gaza
news.summary: At least nine people were reported to have been killed in southern Lebanon on Thursday, as ongoing hostilities continue to exact a heavy toll on civilians and drive a worsening humanitarian crisis marked by rising hunger and strained public services.

news.headline: Hormuz crisis strangling global economy, Guterres warns, demanding solutions to end stalemate
news.summary: The escalating crisis in the Strait of Hormuz could push tens of millions into poverty, trigger a surge in global hunger and even tip the world towards recession, the UN Secretary-General warned on Thursday.

news.headline: Sudanese journalists awarded UNESCO press freedom prize
news.summary: An independent organization for journalists in Sudan has been honoured for its commitment “to deliver accurate, lifesaving information” amid the ongoing civil war, the UN educational and cultural agency UNESCO announced on Thursday.&nbsp;

news.headline: DPRK Korea: Continued militarisation a ‘serious concern’, political affairs chief warns Security Council
news.summary: The continued pursuit of nuclear weapons and ballistic missile development by The Democratic People’s Republic of Korea remain “a matter of serious concern,” the UN’s political affairs chief told the Security Council on Thursday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Central Asia celebrates 20 years as a nuclear-weapon-free zone
news.summary: Semipalatinsk, Kazakhstan was once the Soviet Union’s primary testing ground for nuclear weapons. Today, in an age of rising nuclear threats, the Semipalatinsk Treaty – which saw a group of Central Asian countries renounce nuclear weapons in 2006 – is more relevant than ever.

news.headline: UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict
news.summary: The UN’s top human rights official warned on Wednesday that Iranians’ rights are being eroded in “harsh and brutal ways,” citing a surge in executions, mass arrests and alleged abuses amid a widening crackdown on dissent during the ongoing conflict.

news.headline: From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks
news.summary: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to send shockwaves through global food systems, the UN Food and Agriculture Organization has warned, as a widening crisis across the Middle East continues to strain humanitarian operations and deepen instability.

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
news.summary: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political affairs official Khaled Khiari warned of increasingly dire co

news.headline: Over half of South Sudan’s population faces acute hunger crisis
news.summary: Conflict and displacement are intensifying South Sudan’s hunger crisis, with&nbsp;7.8 million people facing high levels of acute food insecurity while 2.2 million children are suffering from acute malnutrition, according to&nbsp;a joint statement&nbsp;on Tuesday from UN agencies.

news.headline: Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence'
news.summary: Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement – but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

news.headline: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 women teachers and health workers
news.summary: Restrictions on girls’ education and women’s employment in Afghanistan could leave the country with a deficit of over 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) warned on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council
news.summary: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the Security Council is debating maritime security with the UN chief calling on nations restricting ship m

news.headline: Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns
news.summary: Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance – “an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community,” a senior official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF has said.&nbsp;

news.headline: Mali: Guterres calls for international solutions to curb spread of violent extremism in the Sahel
news.summary: There are reports of continuing clashes in Mali on Sunday, a day after a series of coordinated attacks across the landlocked African nation against Government forces by extremists and northern separatist rebels.

news.headline: A race for rights: How sport is helping protect girls in Uganda
news.summary: On a red running track in eastern Uganda, coach Zuena Cheptoek is doing more than training runners. For many girls in the Sebei subregion, she is also a confidante, a mentor and first line of protection against female genital mutilation, child marriage and abuse.

news.headline: UN rights chief urges action to end impunity and address disappearances in Mexico
news.summary: Mexican authorities must intensify efforts to end impunity, protect journalists and human rights defenders, and address the country’s “painful” crisis of disappearances, UN human rights chief Volker Türk has said.

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
news.summary: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operations including by the UN-backed&nbsp;Gang Suppression Force

news.headline: Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal
news.summary: Libya’s political leaders continue to lag in implementing a roadmap that leads to national elections and unified institutions, the UN Special Representative for the country told the Security Council on Wednesday.

news.headline: Earth Day: The battle to save a drowning Pacific island nation
news.summary: As the Pacific island nation of Tuvalu battles rising sea levels that threaten to put large parts of the island entirely underwater by the end of this century, its citizens are making efforts to safeguard their future while preparing for the worst impacts of climate change.&nbsp;

news.headline: Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages
news.summary: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.

news.headline: As Colombia elections near, Security Council hears calls for calm
news.summary: While hope for peace is alive in Colombia as the country prepares for presidential elections, the Security Council on Tuesday heard that achieving it – and making it last – still requires the full implementation of a peace accord signed almost 10 years ago.

news.headline: World News in Brief: AI diagnostics, humanitarian deal for DR Congo, rights abuse allegations in Belarus, Ukraine children bear heaviest burden
news.summary: New data shows that nearly&nbsp;three in four countries in Europe now use Artificial Intelligence in their health services to make a diagnosis.

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks
news.summary: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March

news.headline: Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on
news.summary: Across war-torn Sudan, women and girls “are telling a consistent story of continued experience of danger, and risks for gender-based violence” whether when fleeing to safety or arriving at displacement camps, a senior official with the UN reproductive and sexual health agency UNFPA said on Friday.

news.headline: ‘Slaughtered like goats’: Despair and abandonment in South Sudan
news.summary: Senior UN officials painted a sorry picture of South Sudan on Friday at the Security Council, describing political turmoil, rising violence, hunger and disease, amid budget cuts that are limiting the ability of the UN peacekeeping mission to protect civilians.

news.headline: Record number of Rohingya refugees died at sea in 2025: UNHCR
news.summary: In 2025, nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal, making it the deadliest year on record in South and Southeast Asia, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, said on Friday.

news.headline: More than half of Haitians continue to face food crisis
news.summary: 5.8 million Haitians, or roughly 52 per cent of the population, are facing crisis levels of food insecurity, or worse. Of those, more than 1.8 million are dealing with emergency levels, which means they are exhausting their last assets and unable to meet even basic food needs.

news.headline: Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar
news.summary: Six weeks since war erupted in the Middle East, the shockwaves have spread to the Caribbean region, already pushed to the brink, amid fears of a looming El Niño-linked climate disaster.

news.headline: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
news.summary: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.

news.headline: The Epstein files: Rights experts demand accountability, call for probe into trafficking allegations
news.summary: UN independent human rights experts&nbsp;called on Thursday for justice and accountability for young women and girls who were trafficked systematically as part of allegations contained in the so-called Epstein files.

news.headline: ‘Sudan is an atrocities laboratory’, UN aid chief tells Berlin conference
news.summary: Foreign ministers from across the world met in Berlin on Wednesday to show support for Sudan, where a fourth year of brutal warfare has begun as humanitarian needs deepen.&nbsp;

news.headline: Africa: Nearly 20 million measles deaths averted since 2000
news.summary: Measles vaccinations have saved nearly 20 million lives in Africa since the year 2000 and more than 500 million children were protected through routine immunisation, but the continent remains offtrack in the fight against vaccine-preventable diseases.&nbsp;

news.headline: Middle East conflict chokes end of supply chain as lights go out in the Pacific
news.summary: For Pacific Island countries, the Middle East crisis is not a distant geopolitical event. It is already showing up in higher fuel prices, electricity uncertainty and fears that communities sitting at the far end of global supply chains could be pushed into deeper economic insecurity.

news.headline: Tobago’s teen archery champion takes aim at sustainability targets
news.summary: Six-time Trinidad and Tobago archery champion, Anthurium Lewis, has told UN News how sport helped her overcome age barriers in environmental advocacy and how in the future it can contribute to reaching globally agreed poverty and sustainability goals.

news.headline: Tensions soar after Kim’s latest act - News.com.au
news.summary: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi8wFBVV95cUxNZDNRT3piMmt6WEhzQ1lpMXFUOHZZRE5FRDNyUlpvbERoeUF0cHhZeFZicWIwNTJuX242U1Boa25IdzRXZkxnWFZfSmpDVWozaDZTUGdRbHVMb2puMVV1bzdUWlJEU1pBb0FvNWphT0hpWDdnbkVKT01hN0o3dmtvbzlsdnhxcXEtYWd6bE45TjFyQU5Idkp5Qmp1WTZpSGRUTlBZOVZLUWg4RDlMVGdXRWpWaWVxZ29

news.headline: European Union support for the UN ‘more vital than ever’, Security Council hears
news.summary: A senior United Nations official on Monday hailed the European Union (EU) as “a major economic and diplomatic actor and a strong advocate of multilateralism.”&nbsp;&nbsp;

news.headline: From conflict to catwalk: Women ex-combatants weave reconciliation in Colombia
news.summary: Katerine Avella is a former combatant in Colombia’s decades-long civil war, a peace signatory and a community leader. After the guns fell silent, she created the fashion brand Ixora but, with violence returning to the region, Ms. Avella is now focusing on trying to keep the project afloat in the fac

news.headline: Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence
news.summary: Haiti is facing “one of the most severe and rapidly deteriorating humanitarian crises in the Western Hemisphere,” a senior UN aid official warned on Friday, underscoring the need for continued global attention to alleviate suffering there.&nbsp;

news.headline: Kosovo mission head warns ‘mistrust’ threatens post-election stability
news.summary: Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a “delicate equilibrium” persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.

news.headline: North Korea Says Its Latest Weapons Tests Included Missiles with Cluster-Bomb Warheads - Military.com
